chapter_title.en: Chapter 33 – Cutting the hair and the shaving

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/379)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/379)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ النَّاسَ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّ حَلْقَ الرَّأْسِ مُثْلَةٌ فَقَالَ عُمْرَةٌ لَنَا وَ مُثْلَةٌ لِأَعْدَائِنَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, raising it, said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> that the people are saying that shaving-off the head is a mutilation. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It is an Umrah for us<sup>asws</sup> and a mutilation for our<sup>asws</sup> enemies’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from in a marfu‘ manner the following: “I once said to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘People say shaving of the head is like causing deformity.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It is like performing al-‘Umrah for us and deformity for our enemies.’”
